failure_surface: Output cells were placed at I2 instead of the instructed I3 starting position, causing format/content mismatch with evaluation expectations
root_cause: Agent prioritized the answer_position metadata field ('I2:J7') over the explicit natural language instruction stating 'put from I3', resulting in a 1-row offset error in header and data placement
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When natural language instructions explicitly specify a starting cell position (e.g., 'from I3'), that specification takes precedence over range metadata; verify the exact starting row/column before writing any values
evidence: Instruction explicitly states: 'The answer should be put from I3 with the Headers'
Agent code placed headers at row 2: ws.cell(row=row_offset - 1 + 1, column=9, value='ITEM') where row_offset = 2 results in row 2, not row 3
Evaluation returned test_case_results: [0] indicating complete failure despite successful code execution without errors
answer_position metadata showed 'I2:J7' which conflicted with the explicit instruction saying I3

memory_items.title: Prioritize Explicit Starting Cell Instructions Over Range Metadata
memory_items.lesson: When task instructions specify an exact starting cell (e.g., 'from I3'), always use that position even if range metadata suggests different boundaries
memory_items.generalization: Explicit natural language cell position directives take precedence over range specifications that may indicate the maximum affected area rather than the starting point

memory_items.title: Verify Start Position Before Writing Headers and Data
memory_items.lesson: Before writing headers and data rows, confirm the exact starting row number from the instruction text matches the calculated row_offset in code
memory_items.generalization: Calculate and log the intended start position before execution to catch off-by-one errors early in the coding phase

memory_items.title: Apply Formatting Across Complete Specified Range Including Headers
memory_items.lesson: When instructions require alignment or formatting for 'entire answer range', include all written cells (headers and data) within that formatting loop
memory_items.generalization: Ensure formatting operations cover the full range from the first header cell through all data rows to maintain consistent presentation

edits.content:
### 7. Position Verification
- **Starting Cell Priority**: When instructions explicitly specify a starting cell (e.g., 'put from I3'), that directive takes precedence over range metadata boundaries.
- **Offset Validation**: Always calculate and verify the target row/column matches the exact instruction before writing headers or data.
- **Off-by-One Check**: Confirm whether row numbers in code are 0-indexed (pandas/numpy) or 1-indexed (openpyxl) to avoid position errors.
